title: Greece's Groundbreaking Constitutional AI Safeguards
description: Greece is set to introduce constitutional amendments to ensure artificial intelligence serves humanity and democratic governance. Conservative Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis emphasized the importance of safeguarding future generations through these revisions. The changes also include educational reforms and a potential social media age restriction policy.
content: In a significant move, Greece is preparing major constitutional amendments aimed at ensuring artificial intelligence serves human society and supports democratic governance. Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis highlighted the global concerns surroundi... [776 chars]

title: EU progressing ban on AI ‘nudification’ tools
description: Artificial intelligence “nudification” tools that can generate child sexual abuse material and explicit images of adults without their consent are set to be banned at the EU.
content:
European Parliament and European Council negotiators agreed on Thursday to ban such AI systems, which will need to be formally adopted before it can enter into law.

title: AI Innovators Across Industries: Momentum builds ahead of ET Most Innovative AI Product Awards 2026
description: As artificial intelligence reshapes industries and business operations, the ET Most Innovative AI Product Awards 2026 recognise organisations building impactful, scalable, and future-ready AI solutions. With the early bird offer ending on May 10, the platform presents a limited-time opportunity for startups, SMEs, and enterprises to showcase their innovations on one of Indias leading AI recognition platforms. Spanning 11 categories, the awards celebrate breakthrough AI products driving measurable business value across sectors.

title: Greece, birthplace of democracy, seeks to put humanity ahead of AI in updated constitution
description: Greece is preparing sweeping constitutional reforms that would require artificial intelligence to serve human society and protect individual freedoms. Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis announced the proposed amendment Thursday as governments worldwide debate AI regulation and democratic oversight. The revisions would also expand postal voting, extend mandatory schooling and ban retroactive taxation. Greek officials say the AI provision would act as a long-term safeguard as technology platforms gain greater influence over daily life. The reforms must pass votes in two successive parliaments, a process that typically requires cross-party backing in Greece’s legislature.
content:
ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Greece is preparing major constitutional changes, including an amendment requiring that artificial intelligence serve human society.
